title: What Lies in the Woods
author: Kate Alice Marshall
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "What Lies in the Woods" book by Kate Alice Marshall?
Options:
A. No worries, that would provide me more time to return to Chester after. I need to take this call. I'll call you right back, alright? I pushed open the door with my shoulder and entered the room. I'm not leaving. We'll speak later. I ended the call and closed the door behind me with my foot, then typed a response as I went deeper into the room. The shadow was in the periphery of my vision. I didn't see it until it shifted - and in that moment I realized it was a man, jumping towards me. A man with brown hair and an icy look in his eyes.
B. No problem, that would give me extra time to go back to Chester afterward. I have to answer this. I'll ring you back in just a second, okay? I used my shoulder to open the door and walked into the room. I'm staying put. We'll talk later. I hung up and shut the door behind me with my foot, then typed out a reply as I moved further into the room. The shadow was in the edge of my vision. I didn't notice it until it moved- and in that instant I saw it was a man, lunging at me. A man with brown hair and a cold expression in his eyes.  
C. No problemthat would give me more time to get back to Chester afterward. Ive got to answer this. Ill call you back in just a sec, okay? I shouldered the door open and stepped into the room. Im not going anywhere. Talk to you later. I hung up and shut the door behind me with my foot, then typed out a reply as I moved farther into the room. The shadow was in my peripheral vision. I didnt notice it until it movedand in the same instant I realized it was a man, lunging toward me. A man with brown hair and a cold look in his eyes.
D. No worries, that would allow me more time to return to Chester later. I need to take this call. I'll phone you back in a moment, alright? I pushed the door open with my shoulder and entered the room. I'm not leaving. We'll chat later. I ended the call and closed the door behind me with my foot, then typed a response as I went deeper into the room. The shadow was in the corner of my eye. I didn't see it until it shifted- and at the same moment I realized it was a man, jumping at me. A man with brown hair and an icy look in his eyes.
Answer:
